thinking.text:
I see the script is still running. The output shows many overlapping/noisy commands because each frame captures the same text with slight OCR variations. I need a completely different approach. 

Let me think about this differently. Instead of OCR-ing every second, I should:
1. Extract frames only when the screen changes (detect scene changes)
2. Or better: extract a single frame, OCR all the text, then track what commands were entered by looking at the game transcript (the text that scrolls on screen)

Actually, looking at the Zork output, the game shows all previous commands and their results. So each frame shows the entire history. I could just OCR a frame near the end of the video and it would show ALL the commands the player entered.

Let me wait for the script to finish, then examine a few key frames.
